kernels.label: 12-TET
kernels.notes: Direct formal abstraction of the twelve-tone equal-tempered pitch grid. This kernel defines frequency permission only; it does not imply any timbre, instrument, or repertoire.
kernels.provenance: PENUMBRA design spec v10 section 7 fixed topology; formal 12-TET interval grid.

kernels.label: Maqam (abstract grid)

modes.label: Rast-like abstract subset
modes.notes: Review-required interval subset; named as an orientation, not an authenticity claim.

modes.label: Bayati-like abstract subset
modes.notes: Review-required interval subset; named as an orientation, not an authenticity claim.

modes.label: Hijaz-like abstract subset
modes.notes: Review-required interval subset; named as an orientation, not an authenticity claim.

kernels.notes: Intentional abstraction of a denser non-12-TET pitch-permission grid. It remains provisional and requires human review before any stronger claim is made.
kernels.provenance: PENUMBRA design spec v10 fixed geographic topology plus implementation placeholder from provisional candidate grid.

kernels.label: Indian tunings (abstract grid)

modes.label: Dawn abstract subset
modes.notes: Review-required subset; preserves the idea of time-linked pitch selection without claiming a specific raga.

modes.label: Day abstract subset
modes.notes: Review-required subset; preserves time linkage as a structural abstraction.

modes.label: Night abstract subset
modes.notes: Review-required subset; preserves time linkage as a structural abstraction.

kernels.notes: Intentional abstraction inspired by dense pitch-grid thinking. It does not model a specific raga, school, instrument, or performance practice.
kernels.provenance: PENUMBRA design spec v10 fixed geographic topology plus provisional implementation candidate intervals requiring later curation.

kernels.label: Slendro / Pelog (abstract grid)

modes.label: Slendro abstract subset
modes.notes: Review-required subset; not a claim of ensemble tuning accuracy.

modes.label: Pelog abstract subset
modes.notes: Review-required subset; not a claim of ensemble tuning accuracy.

kernels.notes: Intentional combined abstraction. The kernel remains a pitch-permission surface only and must not imply gamelan timbre or repertoire.
kernels.provenance: PENUMBRA design spec v10 fixed geographic topology plus provisional implementation candidate grid.

kernels.label: East Asian pentatonic

modes.label: Yang pentatonic abstraction
modes.notes: 12-TET subset used as a formal pentatonic selector.

modes.label: Yin pentatonic abstraction
modes.notes: 12-TET subset used as a formal pentatonic selector.

kernels.notes: Author-position scale kernel. The asset distinguishes East Asian pentatonic structures without claiming to represent a full cultural music system.
kernels.provenance: PENUMBRA design spec v10 section 7.6 authorial-position decision and fixed topology.

kernels.label: Church modes
kernels.notes: Direct formal modal abstraction within 12-TET. It defines scale selection only and does not imply liturgical timbre or repertoire.
kernels.provenance: PENUMBRA design spec v10 fixed topology and conventional 12-TET modal interval subsets.

kernels.label: West African + blues inflection

modes.label: Pentatonic + blue inflection abstraction
modes.notes: Review-required selector; not a claim of specific regional practice.

kernels.notes: Intentional scale-selector abstraction. It remains provisional to avoid unsupported authenticity claims across diverse traditions.
kernels.provenance: PENUMBRA design spec v10 fixed geographic topology plus provisional implementation candidate subsets.

kernels.label: Andean pentatonic
kernels.notes: Intentional scale-selector abstraction that keeps the spec topology without representing Andean music as a whole.
kernels.provenance: PENUMBRA design spec v10 fixed geographic topology plus provisional implementation candidate subsets.
